name: Riverside Kedgeree
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Cold cooked salmon or trout
recipeInstructions:
Note: Salmon, sea trout, or river trout may be used. (Farmed trout is unsuitable - its texture is too soft).
Peel and seed the cucumber and cut the flesh into pieces no larger than matchsticks. Sprinkle with 1/2 teaspoon each salt and caster sugar and 1 teaspoon tarragon vinegar. Toss lightly and set aside for at least 40 minutes, or all day if you prefer, to draw out some of the cucumber juices. Drain well and pat dry.
Cook the rice in double its volume of boiling salted water or, better still, in fish stock. It will take about 35 minutes to become tender. Towards the end of this time cook the vegetables. Boil the peas in a little lightly sugared water. Melt the butter in a flameproof casserole, add the cucumber pieces and cook over medium-low heat for 2 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Break the skinned and boned fish into large flaky chunks. Lay the fish on top of the cucumber mixture - do not stir it in. Cover the casserole and reduce heat as low as possible. Leave to cook, just shaking the casserole occasionally, until the cucumber is tender and the fish is well heated through (about 4 minutes).
Meanwhile put the watercress into a food processor and reduce it to green flecks.
To assemble the kedgeree, add the prepared watercress, peas, and cooked rice to the buttery fish and cucumber mixture. Immediately remove from heat and season generously with sea salt and freshly ground black pepper. Toss gently but thoroughly to mix well and serve immediately with a large fresh green salad or tomato salad. Serves 4-6.
